hooks.comment: Require [BACKUP] commit before any file edit — enforces Pre-Change Protocol. BLOCKS on miss (exit 2). Accepts only [BACKUP] (per self-audit finding 1: [CHANGE] bypassed gate).
hooks.command: bash -c 'GIT_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null) || exit 0; LAST=$(git -C "$GIT_ROOT" log -1 --pretty=%s 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$LAST" ] && exit 0; echo "$LAST" | grep -qE "^\[BACKUP\]" && exit 0; echo "=== PRE-CHANGE PROTOCOL REQUIRED ===" >&2; echo "Last commit is NOT a [BACKUP] commit." >&2; echo "Last commit was: $LAST" >&2; echo "Run first:" >&2; echo "  git add <files> && git commit -m \"[BACKUP] Pre-change: <description> | Risks: <list> | Scope: <files>\"" >&2; echo "See: .claude/rules/workflow.md" >&2; exit 2'

hooks.comment: Config protection — block accidental edits to CLAUDE.md and settings.json without explicit user request
hooks.command:
bash -c 'f="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH"; base=$(basename "$f"); if [[ "$base" == "CLAUDE.md" || "$base" == "settings.json" ]] && [[ "$f" == *".claude/"* || "$f" == *"/CLAUDE.md" ]]; then echo "[PROTECTED] $base is a critical config file.
Ask the user to confirm before editing."; exit 2; fi'

hooks.comment: Require [PREFIX] on all git commit messages (per self-audit finding 2). Allowed: BACKUP, CHANGE, META, HANDOFF, PROCESS, RULES, SEC, FIX. Blocks (exit 2) on missing prefix.
hooks.command: bash -c 'cmd="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_COMMAND"; if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE "git\s+commit\s"; then msg=$(echo "$cmd" | sed -nE "s/.*-m[[:space:]]+[\"'\''](.*)[\"'\''].*/\1/p" | head -1); if [ -n "$msg" ] && ! echo "$msg" | grep -qE "^\[(BACKUP|CHANGE|META|HANDOFF|PROCESS|RULES|SEC|FIX)\]"; then echo "=== COMMIT PREFIX REQUIRED ===" >&2; echo "Message: $msg" >&2; echo "Required prefix: [BACKUP|CHANGE|META|HANDOFF|PROCESS|RULES|SEC|FIX]" >&2; echo "See: .claude/rules/workflow.md (commit taxonomy)" >&2; exit 2; fi; fi'

hooks.comment: Block destructive shell commands — rm -rf, git init, git reset --hard, git push --force
hooks.command:
bash -c 'cmd="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_COMMAND"; if echo "$cmd" | grep -qiE "rm[[:space:]]+-rf|rm[[:space:]]+-[a-z]*r[a-z]*f|git[[:space:]]+init|git[[:space:]]+reset[[:space:]]+--hard|git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+--force|git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+-f[[:space:]]"; then echo "[BLOCKED] Destructive shell command detected.
Command: $cmd
If this is intentional, ask the user to run it manually."; exit 2; fi'

hooks.comment: Block destructive SQL — DROP TABLE, TRUNCATE, DROP DATABASE, DELETE without WHERE
hooks.command:
bash -c 'cmd="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_COMMAND"; if echo "$cmd" | grep -qiE "DROP[[:space:]]+(TABLE|DATABASE|SCHEMA)|TRUNCATE[[:space:]]+TABLE|TRUNCATE[[:space:]]+[a-z_]+"; then echo "[BLOCKED] Destructive SQL detected.
Command: $cmd
If this is intentional, ask the user to run it manually."; exit 2; fi'

hooks.comment: Warn on DELETE without WHERE clause (data loss risk)
hooks.command:
bash -c 'cmd="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_COMMAND"; if echo "$cmd" | grep -qiE "DELETE[[:space:]]+FROM[[:space:]]+[a-z_]+[[:space:]]*;" && ! echo "$cmd" | grep -qi "WHERE"; then echo "[WARN] DELETE without WHERE detected — will erase entire table.
Command: $cmd
Blocked. Add a WHERE clause or run manually."; exit 2; fi'

hooks.comment: TypeScript type-check after .ts/.tsx edit — auto-detects project root via tsconfig.json
hooks.command: bash -c 'f="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH"; [[ "$f" == *.tsx || "$f" == *.ts ]] || exit 0; d=$(dirname "$f"); while [ "$d" != "/" ]; do [ -f "$d/tsconfig.json" ] && { cd "$d" && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20; exit $?; }; d=$(dirname "$d"); done; echo "[SKIP] No tsconfig.json found for $f"'

hooks.comment: Python ruff lint after .py edit — auto-detects project root via pyproject.toml
hooks.command: bash -c 'f="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH"; [[ "$f" == *.py ]] || exit 0; d=$(dirname "$f"); while [ "$d" != "/" ]; do [ -f "$d/pyproject.toml" ] && { cd "$d" && uv run ruff check "$f" 2>&1 | head -10; exit $?; }; d=$(dirname "$d"); done; echo "[SKIP] No pyproject.toml found for $f"'

hooks.comment: Warn if console.log is left in frontend code
hooks.command: bash -c 'f="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH"; if [[ "$f" == *.tsx || "$f" == *.ts || "$f" == *.js ]]; then grep -n "console\.log" "$f" && echo "[WARN] console.log found in $f — remove before deploy" || true; fi'

Stop.comment: Final scan for console.log in project src dirs

hooks.command: bash -c 'GIT_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null) || exit 0; found=$(grep -rn "console\.log" "$GIT_ROOT/src" "$GIT_ROOT/frontend/src" "$GIT_ROOT/app" 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules | head -5); [ -n "$found" ] && echo "$found" && echo "[STOP CHECK] console.log found — clean up before deploy" || true'

Stop.comment: Context budget warning — check session activity and warn before context gets too heavy

hooks.command:
bash -c 'GIT_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null) || exit 0; COMMITS=$(git -C "$GIT_ROOT" log --since="4 hours ago" --oneline 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d " "); if [ "$COMMITS" -gt 4 ]; then echo "[CONTEXT BUDGET] $COMMITS commits this session — past 50% context.
ACTION REQUIRED:
1. Commit current work ([WIP] if not finished)
2. Write docs/handoff.md with exact state
3. Run /compact to free context
4. After compact — read handoff.md and continue
Do NOT start another task before compacting."; elif [ "$COMMITS" -gt 2 ]; then echo "[CONTEXT BUDGET] $COMMITS commits this session (~40% context).
Finish current task, then write docs/handoff.md before starting next task."; fi'

Stop.comment: Session log — append session entry for cost/activity tracking

hooks.command: bash -c 'GIT_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null) || exit 0; LOG="$GIT_ROOT/.claude/session-log.jsonl"; COMMITS=$(git -C "$GIT_ROOT" log --since="4 hours ago" --oneline 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d " "); FILES=$(git -C "$GIT_ROOT" diff --stat HEAD~${COMMITS:-1} 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | tr -d " "); echo "{\"ts\":\"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\",\"commits\":$COMMITS,\"summary\":\"$FILES\"}" >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null; echo "[SESSION LOGGED] $COMMITS commits this session → .claude/session-log.jsonl"'

PreCompact.comment: Auto-checkpoint before /compact — captures observable state to docs/handoff/ so context loss is bounded
